message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #4 ready for review, need 9/9">
Task #4 (HTMX empty state transition) committed and pushed (4e049aa). CI is running.

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review and send consent or concerns. We need 9/9 consensus.

Note: Carson pushed before receiving the process update — that's fine. The new flow (consensus before push) applies starting with Task #5.
</teammate-message>

content.content:
commit 4e049aa51c00ac6e0983793d1fd091ee7ab9191c
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:37:58 2026 -0800

    Add HTMX empty state transition when last todo is deleted
    
    The empty state message ("No todos yet.") is now always present in the
    DOM with id="todo-empty", hidden via the hidden attribute when items
    exist. The todo-focus.js script toggles visibility after HTMX delete
    and add swaps, so the empty state appears when the last item is
    deleted and disappears when the first item is added.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 static/js/todo-focus.js | 53 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++----
 templates/todos.html    |  4 +---
 tests/api/todos.rs      | 21 ++++++++++++++++----
 3 files changed, 67 insertions(+), 11 deletions(-)

content.text: Now I have the full picture. Let me send my review.

input.content:
**Review of 4e049aa -- HTMX empty state transition** **I consent.** Detailed review: **1. Template change is correct and follows the always-in-DOM pattern.** The empty state `

` was previously conditionally rendered with `{% if todos.is_empty() %}`. Now it is always in the DOM with `id="todo-empty"` and toggles via the `hidden` attribute. This is the same pattern we use for error elements (`add-todo-error`) -- always present, visibility controlled by attribute. The server-rendered initial state is correct: `hidden` when `!todos.is_empty()`, visible when empty. **2. No Rust changes needed -- correct.** The template logic change (`{% if !todos.is_empty() %} hidden{% endif %}`) is purely in Askama. The Rust handler passes the same `TodosTemplate` struct with the same `todos` vec. The `hidden` attribute toggle is handled entirely by the template and JS. **3. `updateEmptyState()` logic is sound.** It queries the actual DOM state (`todoList.querySelector(".todo-item") !== null`) rather than maintaining a counter, which is robust against any ordering edge cases. The function toggles both the `hidden` attribute on `#todo-empty` (the message) and on `.todo-list` (the `

    `), keeping them in sync. **4. Delete path: `pendingDeleteSwap` flag is clean.** Set in `beforeSwap`, consumed in `afterSettle`. This ensures `updateEmptyState()` runs after the DOM has settled from the delete swap. No stale state risk since the flag is reset immediately. **5. Add path: `htmx:afterRequest` on the add form.** Detects successful add by checking `e.detail.elt` has class `todo-add` and `e.detail.successful`. After the new `
  • ` is appended (via `hx-swap="beforeend"`), `updateEmptyState()` correctly hides the empty message and shows the list. This handles the transition from "empty list" to "first item added" without a page reload. **6. Tests updated correctly.** Two existing tests were updated: - `get_todos_shows_empty_state_for_new_user`: Now verifies `id="todo-empty"` exists and is NOT hidden when the list is empty. - `get_todos_shows_added_items`: Now verifies `id="todo-empty"` is always in the DOM but IS hidden when items exist. Both test the server-rendered initial state, which is correct. The JS toggling is a progressive enhancement on top. **7. Hidden `
      ` when empty.** The list has `hidden` when empty (template line 40). The JS mirrors this: when the last item is deleted, the `
        ` gets `hidden` and the message gets `hidden` removed. When the first item is added, the reverse. This prevents an empty `
          ` from being visible (which could show borders/padding from CSS). No concerns. Clean implementation that builds naturally on the Task #3 foundation.
input.summary: Consent on 4e049aa - empty state transition reviewed

content.text: Consent sent for Task #4 (4e049aa). The always-in-DOM pattern with `hidden` attribute toggling is consistent with our existing approach for error elements, and the `updateEmptyState()` function is robust by querying actual DOM state. Standing by for Task #5 (HTMX inline editing), which will follow the new consensus-before-push pipeline.
